item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Robert has informed me this is a reference to something called homestuck

Take my upvote you glorious bastard

item.title: Licensing Junior Staff Post - Info
item.content:encoded: As no source that is compliant with our license could be found for the image in this article, it has been removed. The file is still available at bottom of the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved this SCP. The story of that girl who spent over a week and was thinking of jumping just felt so real. But I appear to be slightly confused about the ending. So she jumped, but I can't tell if she survived. I think she did because it says something about landing safely, but it doesn't talk about her some more.

But in conclusion, this was awesome and I couldn't stop reading it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is so good i want to make this a fucking movie

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohh good point. I’ll probably go back and fix that when I get to my computer at home.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I rather like this. It is unpretentious and clear, and I think the exploration log is quite nicely paced. It's also pretty horrifying that it baits the suicidal into committing suicide by presenting no other option (bar the likely quite small proportion of people like the protagonist).

Just wondering if a civilian would refer to an unknown force or god as an "entity" though? Feels a little clinical/Foundationy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its an alright SCP. The way it was written and how the diary was written, and how it all kept relatively relevant and interesting was great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is a good story. It seems sad as it goes on, but then the person who wrote in the journal jumps and lands safely!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a fan of this one. Up until the final addendum I wasn't sold but the character journey is what sold it for me. Upvoted.

item.title: Nice one!
item.content:encoded:
Now THIS SCP is good, it's pretty much a similarity to 087 but much more fascinating…love it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can’t be 100% sure but I did give it some thought during the initial brainstorming process. I feel like such a life changing experience like this wouldn’t be so easily forgotten, even if it isn’t explicitly remembered. I believe that SCP-3964-2-A wouldn’t have gotten over her issues completely but there will be that feeling of profound change within her that could become a positive change in her actual life.
The fact is she wasn’t planning to go through with a suicide in the first place and the only real reason she would do so was because the skip trapped her in an inescapable situation. There really nothing there for the ethics committee to intervene with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm left wondering… Did the Foundation give her amnestics, thus making her forget she overcame her suicidal ideation, or did the ethics committee intervene?

item.title: I like it
item.content:encoded: This scp hooked me I like it you did good with your grammar and how you wrote it I haven’t seen a scp that hooked me for a long time I mean I’m kinda new to but before I had an account I always tried to find ones like these

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my first SCP so it's still very much a learning experience. I would really like to thank DoctorNovakaine from the critique forum and stormbreath from thecritters chat. Both of them offered amazing and helpful feedback which I greatly incorporated into this final(?) post.
But feel free to be as critical of this as you wish. Like I said, it's a learning experience. I want to know what I can improve upon and fix.

Interesting note: I got into this SCP stuff while working at my school’s library. And it so happens that we have a stairwell that used to go downstairs as well is up, but the downstairs part was bricked off for unexplained reasons (which is honestly ridiculous because now everyone has to physically leave the building, use an outside stairwell and round the building just to get to the downstairs room). Having been absorbed into this fandom-verse and with a slightly overactive imagination this idea was formed. Thus my first page.
